articles.title: BLOCKCHAIN.COM HAS HELD TALKS TO GO PUBLIC VIA SPAC
articles.summary: Blockchain.com has held talks about going public via a SPAC listing with Cohen & Company Capital Markets advising on a potential deal, though it's unclear if discussions are ongoing. The crypto exchange and wallet provider's valuation history shows significant volatility, rising from $5.2 billion post-money valuation in March 2021 to $14 billion in 2022 before declining to $7 billion in November 2023's $110 million funding round. The company is signaling progress by appointing former Goldman Sachs executive Justin Evans as CFO and ex-Point72 portfolio manager Mike Wilcox as COO.

articles.title: ROBINHOOD TOKENIZES NEARLY 500 US STOCKS, ETFS ON ARBITRUM
articles.summary: Robinhood has deployed 493 tokenized assets worth over $8.5 million on Arbitrum, adding 80 new stock tokens in recent days, including Galaxy, Webull, and Synopsys, with stocks accounting for 70% of tokens and ETFs representing 24%. The tokens are structured as blockchain-based derivatives regulated under the EU's MiFID II rather than representing direct share ownership, offering 24-hour market access, no hidden fees beyond 0.1% FX charges, and a 1 euro minimum investment, though it has drawn scrutiny from Lithuania's banking regulator, who has requested structural clarification.

articles.title: INTRODUCING PRIVATE MULTISIG
articles.summary: A privacy-preserving multisig wallet implementation combines Merkle tree membership proofs for participant anonymity, aggregated ECC ElGamal encryption for confidential vote casting with bias-free decision making, and Distributed Key Generation eliminating centralized trusted entities, though requiring all participants to vote before revealing outcomes. The system uses babyJubJub key pairs derived from ECDSA signatures via deterministic KDF rather than wallet addresses to reduce identification probability from 1 to 1/N where N is member count, while homomorphic vote aggregation allows summing encrypted votes without decrypting individual ballots until the final decryption key share completes. Limitations include the last voter advantage, where final participants can decrypt prior votes before casting their own, proposal frontrunning possibilities despite deterministic challenge derivation, participant removal deadlocks if removed members don't vote, causing proposals to expire, and linear scalability complexity for vote revelation.

articles.title: EXPLAINING GKR FOR FAST HASH-PROVING
articles.summary: The GKR protocol turns large “batch and layers” computations like Poseidon2 hashing into simple polynomial evaluations, letting provers commit only to inputs and outputs. With tricks like reducing rounds, batching linear layers, and optimizing sumchecks, GKR achieves around 15x theoretical overhead and under 10x in practice, potentially reaching single-digit overhead. This proving system is the basis behind proposals to reduce the time required to verify Ethereum blocks.

articles.title: COLLECTIVE INTELLIGENCE AND PREDICTION MARKETS
articles.summary: Prediction markets transform collective intelligence into a structured, incentivized system where people are rewarded for accurate insights. Unlike open-source projects or Wikipedia, which rely on voluntary contributions, prediction markets aggregate diverse information through financial stakes, encouraging better, data-driven forecasts. By giving experts and informed outsiders monetary incentives to share knowledge, these markets create more accurate crowd predictions. There are remaining challenges around liquidity and market diversity, but crypto-based prediction platforms are closing the gap between human collaboration and actionable forecasting.

articles.title: ETHEREUM'S “SUPERCYCLE” NARRATIVE
articles.summary: Ethereum's open design enabled speculative waves (ICOs and NFTs) that drained large amounts of ETH from long-term holders to grifters and retail, creating persistent sell pressure that slowed appreciation. With those excesses largely behind it and institutional demand growing, ETH is entering a “supercycle” marked by steadier, less euphoric growth and stronger performance during market corrections.

articles.title: SOLANA'S $500M STABLECOIN LEAK
articles.summary: Solana's $14.5B in USDC and USDT supply generates roughly $580M in annual interest for Circle and Tether, none of which returns to Solana. This interest, reinvested by issuers and their partners like Coinbase and Base, effectively subsidizes rival ecosystems while Solana earns nothing. Unless Solana coordinates around yield-bearing and ecosystem-aligned stable assets, it will continue to fund competitors' growth.

articles.title: AAVE'S IPFS ADVANTAGE DURING CLOUD OUTAGES
articles.summary: During an AWS and Vercel outage, Aave's team reminded users that the Aave front-end is hosted on IPFS and can also be run locally from GitHub, ensuring uninterrupted access even when centralized services go down.

articles.title: HEDGING CRYPTO GAINS WITH POLYMARKET
articles.summary: By betting NO on “stretch” targets like Bitcoin hitting $150K when odds heavily favor YES, holders earn extra yield if prices stay flat, and still win if prices rise since their coins appreciate.

articles.title: AI TRADING SHOWDOWN GOES VIRAL
articles.summary: A developer launched a live crypto trading contest between top AI models (Grok, GPT, DeepSeek, Claude, and Gemini) to see which performs best in real markets.

articles.title: AI IS THE MARKET'S CYCLE DRIVER
articles.summary: The entire market cycle now hinges on AI.
